updates.name: i-Constructibility (Strict Grammar)
updates.description: Under strict principal-branch EML grammar (ln defined only on ℝ⁺, terminal {1}), prove or disprove that i = √(−1) is constructible.
updates.solution_summary: T19 (proved): 3-line induction. Strict grammar types all values as ℝ. Since i ∉ ℝ, i is not constructible. Lean verified in S74 (0 sorries).
